pushDecision.reason: main was ahead of origin/main by 17 commits, not only by the Site Admin Programs commit, so the prompt's safe-push condition was not met.

candidateScan.reason: New real route exists but local demo proof did not cover role boundaries or seeded reversibility.
Viewer landing still pointed at denied sections and violated the no-visible-dead-end rule.
The viewer monitoring panel existed but the real Overview path skipped it, reducing role-specific usability.
Administration is read-only on key site surfaces but the shell did not state that boundary clearly.
Seeded primary site starts with all active programs attached, so first-load add flow is not visible.
Still valuable, but blocked on hosted runtime and safe credentials.
Useful doc pass, but less urgent than fixing the shell and dead-end behavior itself.
Already route-backed and lower risk than Viewer and Administration clarity issues.
Would improve demo shape, but it broadens seeded content and was avoidable this run.
Could remove dead ends, but broadens access and was not necessary to fix the UX safely.
Permission broadening would be riskier than clarifying the existing monitoring boundary.
Too wide for a bounded hourly automation slice.

seededProgramsProof.notes: The primary demo site had no addable program on first load, so the local proof verified a reversible remove and restore cycle instead.

nextRecommendedWorkOrder: Improve the primary local demo seed so Site Admin Programs has an honest first-load add option, then continue the role-readiness refresh across Global Admin, Program Teacher, Mentor, and Student.
